pages.text: In the beginning, there was only the $(l)Transcendence$() — a force older than the world itself. It was neither light nor dark, neither order nor chaos. It simply $(l)was$().$(br2)When the world formed, the Transcendence fractured into eighteen $(l)elemental threads$(), each carrying a fragment of the original power: Fire, Ice, Thunder, Wind, Earth, Void, Holy, Blood, Dark, Light, Poison, Time, Space, Nature, Chaos, Acid, Sonic, and Eldritch.

The $(l)Transcend Alloy$() is the only material capable of containing raw Transcendence. Ancient civilizations discovered that by inscribing patterns — $(l)magic circles$() — into the alloy, they could channel specific elemental threads.$(br2)The art was lost for millennia. What remains are fragments: scattered circle patterns, crystallized mana, and the whispers of a power that once reshaped reality itself.
